description: Native app surfaces phase A3a (docs/design/native-app-surfaces.md): the `android` and `ios` target platforms are validated and capability-gated, but neither runs yet — android devices land in phase A3b, iOS in A4. Every context here must resolve SKIPPED (never FAIL) on every host: a mobile platform names a TARGET, not the host, so it never matches the host platform and instead lands SKIPPED with an actionable, roadmap-shaped reason (install-android when no SDK is found, or the A3b/A4 pointer). This is the asserted gate, not a gap — when phase A3b lands, the android test starts running on capable hosts with no fixture change.

tests.description: An android target context is SKIPPED by the mobile gate. On hosts with no Android SDK the reason points at `doc-detective install android`; on a capable host it points at phase A3b. The startSurface step never runs — the context skips before any step or driver work.
An ios target context is SKIPPED pointing at phase A4 (iOS app surfaces). Validated and gated now so A4 is purely additive.
